narrative_summary: Following a post-ICU pneumonia stay, the patient's transition was initially threatened by a transport vendor cancellation for the Friday discharge. During the Connection Intake, the patient expressed high anxiety regarding the physical move back to her home. By performing the Confidence Audit and verifying HHA 'In Place' status before scheduling the MA, the PN ensured the loop was closed despite the transport friction. The first home visit was successfully completed within the 24-hour window.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: 24-hour pulse call completed. Reached daughter; confirmed oxygen concentrator is active at home. Transport van confirmed for 1400 Friday discharge.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: 'No, I haven't heard of you.' Q2: 'I think you're with the insurance company.' Q3: 'The doctor mentioned someone would come to the house.' Q4: 'I'm worried about the stairs and how I'll get home from the curb.' (Escalated mobility concern to SW).
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm; HHA has the orders. Call 2 (Family): Verified daughter understands the 0900 HHA arrival and confirmed hospital bed was delivered to the first floor.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into daughter's phone during bedside education; program flyer delivered and reviewed with family.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2 Check-in: Patient completing IV antibiotic course for pneumonia. PT reports patient is now at a '1-person assist' for transfers. Target D/C set for Friday.

narrative_summary: Patient Arthur Miller was triaged in Atlantis where a demographic error was corrected before the Connection Intake revealed high levels of Caregiver_Panic from his spouse. During the Maintenance phase, the PN focused on educating the family on the Day 1 transition plan to mitigate discharge refusal. Following the HHA-First Rule, the PN waited for the HHA to confirm a specific nurse and time window before scheduling the MA. The Confidence Audit successfully aligned the SW and the family, leading to a verified home transition despite the initial anxiety.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient and spouse 24 hours prior to D/C. Spouse reported 'last minute cold feet' about the discharge; PN provided de-escalation and re-confirmed the Day 1 support plan.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, first time hearing of the company. Q2: Thinks we are 'extra help' for the nurses. Q3: SW mentioned home health but patient was groggy. Q4: 'My wife is terrified of my chest incision' — identified significant Caregiver_Panic regarding sternal precautions.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm. Call 2 (to Spouse): Conducted Confidence Audit; spouse confirmed she knows the HHA nurse is coming Friday afternoon and has the MA's Saturday morning time slot on her calendar.

narrative_summary: Martha G. was a high-risk renal transition where the Connection Intake revealed significant anxiety regarding home care reliability. The PN successfully built a Confidence Loop by verifying HHA 'In Place' status and conducting a dual-call Confidence Audit. However, on the day of discharge, the HHA nurse failed to appear during the confirmed window (Day 1 Home No-Show). Despite the PN following all HHA-First scheduling rules, the transition failed when the MA arrived at a home where skilled intake had not yet occurred.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient 24 hours prior to D/C. Patient confirmed home is ready and dialysis transport for Monday is booked. Re-confirmed HHA visit time.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: Yes, saw the logo in the lobby. Q2: Thinks we are 'discharge helpers.' Q3: SW mentioned home care but patient was unclear on the MA role. Q4: Expressed high anxiety that the nurse won't show up on time, leaving her alone with her dialysis port — escalated this anxiety to the SW.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm and Reliant HHA has the orders. Call 2 (to daughter): Confirmed daughter will be present for the 10:00 AM HHA window and knows to call the PN if the nurse is 30 minutes late.

narrative_summary: 72yo female with hip fracture ORIF transitioned through the lifecycle with a primary goal of Home. During Stage 1 Connection Intake, the patient identified equipment availability as her primary anxiety, which manifested in Stage 3 as a DME_Delivery_Failure when the walker was missing. Following the HHA-First Rule, I withheld MA scheduling and performed a Deep-Dive HHA Barrier check to identify the specific missing item for the SW. Once the Confidence Audit confirmed the walker was onsite and the HHA was 'In Place', the MA was scheduled, resulting in a successful service adoption.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient 24hrs prior. Confirmed walker is now physically in the home. Patient verbalized understanding of the Day 1 plan and knows to call the HHA if the nurse is delayed.

narrative_summary: Arthur’s transition was initially threatened by 'Caregiver_Panic' from his wife, which I identified during the CONNECTION INTAKE and escalated to the SW. During the MAINTENANCE phase, I focused on family education while navigating a 'HHA_Staffing_Shortage' that delayed the start-of-care. By performing the CONFIDENCE AUDIT and adhering to the HHA-First Rule, I ensured the MA was only scheduled once the HHA was 'In Place,' resulting in a successful home transition.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to discharge. Confirmed that the wife has agreed to the home plan following SW counseling. Home is prepped with the necessary DME.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, family has not heard of the company. Q2: Understands we assist with the transition. Q3: SW provided a brief overview. Q4: Patient is eager to return home, but wife expressed 'Caregiver_Panic' regarding the management of the sternal incision and medication complexity; concern escalated to SW immediately.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed the D/C date is pushed to Monday to ensure HHA coverage. Call 2 (to daughter): Verified understanding of the Day 1 plan, including the arrival of the hospital bed and the HHA nurse's 4-hour arrival window.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into the patient’s daughter’s smartphone; program flyer delivered to the wife at the bedside.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient meeting physical therapy benchmarks. SW indicates a tentative discharge for Friday. Conducted a family education session to walk the wife through Day 1 expectations to mitigate anxiety.

demographic_audit_note: Verified Medicare Advantage Plan; identified and corrected a typo in the primary contact phone number in Atlantis by cross-referencing the facility face sheet. Confirmed home address is within the MA's 30-mile travel radius.

narrative_summary: Patient Maria, a 69-year-old with a diabetic foot ulcer, was successfully transitioned home despite a critical staffing gap. During the Confidence Audit, I identified that the HHA's primary specialist was ill, creating a 'Not In Place' status. By categorizing this as a 'Provider Illness' barrier and escalating to the SW, a bridge nurse was secured, allowing me to safely schedule the MA within the 24-hour window without risking a wasted visit. The Connection Intake early in the stay allowed us to address Maria's clinical anxiety through SW intervention, ensuring a stable handoff.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached patient's daughter 24 hours prior to discharge; confirmed the hospital bed and wound care supplies were delivered to the home. Daughter feels 'confident' with the Day 1 schedule.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: Yes, patient's neighbor used the service. Q2: Thinks we provide the actual wound care. Q3: SW mentioned nursing but not the MA transition visit. Q4: Expressed high anxiety about the frequency of dressing changes; PN escalated this specific concern to the SW to ensure the HHA frequency is clearly defined.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (SW): Confirmed D/C date is firm for Friday morning. Call 2 (Daughter): Confirmed she understands the HHA nurse arrives first for the SOC and the MA will follow within 24 hours to reinforce the care plan.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into patient's smartphone and daughter's phone; program flyer delivered and reviewed at bedside.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Wound showing granular tissue; patient cleared by PT for home discharge. SW targets a Friday discharge. PN confirmed with HHA that the referral was received and logged in the Hub.

The PN identified this environmental barrier during the Connection Intake and maintained alignment with the SW throughout the bureaucratic hold. Once the ramp was installed, the PN executed the Confidence Audit, ensuring the HHA was 'In Place' before scheduling the MA. The transition was successfully completed within 24 hours of discharge.

stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed D/C date is firm for 10/13 following ramp inspection. Call 2 (to daughter): Verified she knows Elena (HHA) is coming Monday morning and knows to call the agency if the nurse hasn't arrived by 11:30 AM.

narrative_summary: Patient Elena Vance was triaged in Atlantis following an MS flare-up with a clear goal for home discharge. During the Connection Intake, she expressed anxiety regarding her IV steroid schedule, which was immediately escalated to the Social Worker. The transition faced a delay when the primary HHA nurse fell ill, but by adhering to the HHA-First Rule, I delayed MA scheduling until a backup nurse was confirmed 'In Place'. The Confidence Loop was closed through a final audit with the SW and family, ensuring a safe handoff for the Saturday start-of-care.

stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday D/C is firm and HHA has confirmed the Saturday 9:00 AM start-of-care. Call 2 (to family): Confirmed daughter understands the Saturday nurse arrival and has the walker ready.

hha_confirmation_status: Not In Place as of Wednesday due to HHA Staffing Shortage in the 19143 zip code. PN performed a Deep Dive and found the HHA intake coordinator had the referral but no nurse was available for a Friday admission. Escalated to SW for alternative agency search. Status updated to 'In Place' on Thursday at 3:45 PM after SW secured coverage and HHA confirmed RN 'Sarah' with a 10:00 AM Friday window.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirmed the goal is Home with 24/7 family supervision and HHA support; LTC transition was discussed but ruled out by POA (daughter).
label: Case_25
ma_visit_booking: MA visit successfully scheduled for Friday at 2:00 PM. Booking was delayed until Thursday evening to ensure the HHA-First Rule was satisfied and a nurse was assigned.
narrative_summary: Arthur Vance (86M, Dementia) was successfully transitioned home following a hip fracture and fall-related hospitalization. During the ENTRY/TRIAGE phase, the Connection Intake revealed significant fall anxiety, which prompted a PN escalation to the SW for an urgent home safety evaluation. Despite a critical HHA Staffing Shortage that threatened to delay discharge, the PN maintained the Confidence Loop by performing a deep-dive barrier check and only scheduling the MA once the HHA was confirmed 'In Place' during the HANDOFF stage.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter via phone 24 hours prior to D/C. Home readiness confirmed; daughter expressed relief that a specific nurse name was provided. No new clinical setbacks reported.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: 'No, I haven't heard of you.' Q2: 'I think you're here to help me walk.' Q3: 'The social worker said a nurse would come to the house.' Q4: 'I am terrified of falling again when my daughter is at work.' (Anxiety regarding fall history escalated to SW for PT home safety priority).
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed Friday 11:00 AM D/C is firm and HHA has assigned a nurse. Call 2 (to daughter): Confirmed daughter understands RN Sarah arrives between 10 AM - 12 PM and MA will follow in the afternoon.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into daughter's phone during bedside meeting; program flyer delivered to patient and taped to discharge folder.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2 Check-in: Patient cognitively fluctuated but cleared by PT for ambulation with a walker. SW target discharge is Friday. Daughter (POA) confirmed home modifications (grab bars) are complete.
